pi_items.source: SQL dump of gnuhealth-50-demo database (party_party table id 35)
SQL dump of gnuhealth-50-demo database (party_party table id 35)
export_result.sh explicitly checks for new gnuhealth_patient_disease records where gpath.code LIKE "W%" OR gpath.code LIKE "S%"
setup_task.sh injects an Enalapril allergy as a distractor for a different patient, and export_result.sh explicitly checks for "%enalapril%" (among other ACE inhibitors) in the new allergy record
export_result.sh explicitly accepts "losartan", "amlodip", and "hydrochlorothiazid" (along with other ARBs, CCBs, and thiazides) as valid safer antihypertensive prescriptions
verifier.py logic explicitly awards full points only if new_lab_count >= 2
export_result.sh enforces a minimum appointment date of TASK_START_DATE + 6 days (buffer to cover exactly 7 days)
export_result.sh enforces a maximum appointment date of TASK_START_DATE + 22 days (buffer to safely cover 21 days)
task.json description states "As the clinical pharmacist" handling a "68-year-old" patient
task.json description context (patient "admitted after a fall")

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on the synthetic GNU Health demo database. The target patient is Roberto Carlos (gnuhealth_patient id 12). The evaluation scripts explicitly require the agent to: 1) document a new diagnosis code starting with "W" or "S" for the fall injury, 2) record an allergy to an ACE inhibitor (Enalapril is explicitly matched in the verifier and used as a distractor for a different patient in the setup script), 3) prescribe a safe antihypertensive alternative (explicitly matching Losartan, Amlodipine, Hydrochlorothiazide, among others) while NOT re-prescribing an ACE inhibitor, 4) order at least 2 new laboratory tests, and 5) schedule a follow-up appointment between 7 and 21 days from the task start date.
